articles.title: Family Entertainment Group Names Jason Clark as New CFO
articles.summary: Family Entertainment Group, one of the nation's largest FEC operators, has appointed Jason Clark as Chief Financial Officer effective May 26, 2026. Clark brings financial leadership experience from the restaurant, hospitality, and entertainment sectors. The hire signals FEG's focus on financial strategy during a period of significant organizational growth.

articles.title: FACE Amusement Group Opens 50,000-Sq-Ft 'Guy Fieri's Downtown Flavortown' in Myrtle Beach
articles.summary: FACE Amusement Group has opened a 50,000-square-foot dining and entertainment destination at Myrtle Beach Mall in partnership with celebrity chef Guy Fieri. The venue expands on the brand's flagship Pigeon Forge location and represents a growing trend of celebrity-branded FEC concepts. Operators should note the large footprint and entertainment-dining hybrid model as a benchmark for experiential anchor venues.

articles.title: Private Equity Firm Periscope Invests in Amusement Connect
articles.summary: Periscope, a private equity firm, has made an investment in Amusement Connect, signaling continued investor interest in FEC technology and management platforms. This deal could accelerate product development and market expansion for Amusement Connect's operator-facing tools. FEC operators and suppliers should watch for new capabilities or pricing changes as the company scales.

articles.title: Urban Air Adventure Park Expands into Maryland with New Indoor Entertainment Location
articles.summary: Urban Air Adventure Park continues its aggressive nationwide growth with a new Maryland location, reinforcing its position as one of the fastest-growing FEC chains in the U.S. The brand's expansion into new markets puts competitive pressure on independent and regional operators. FEC owners in the Mid-Atlantic should take note of the brand's footprint and programming strategy.

articles.title: Urban Air Adventure Park Opens New Location in Salem, Oregon
articles.summary: A new Urban Air Adventure Park has opened in Salem, Oregon, offering a sneak peek at the brand's latest facility design and attraction mix. The opening underscores Urban Air's continued westward expansion into markets previously underserved by large-format indoor FECs. Local and regional FEC competitors should monitor the brand's attraction lineup and pricing model.

articles.title: Brogent Unveils 360-Degree R-Ride at IAAPA Expo Asia, Announces Japanese IP Partnership
articles.summary: Brogent Technologies debuted its R-Ride at IAAPA Expo Asia 2026, a motion theatre featuring a 360-degree panoramic screen and full rotation system running at 8 RPM. The first R-Ride installation is slated for VinWonders Vietnam in Q4 2027, with multiple additional Southeast Asian projects announced. FEC operators and attraction buyers should evaluate this next-generation immersive ride format as a potential anchor experience.

articles.title: Brogent's R-Ride Debut at IAAPA Asia Backed by Japanese IP Deals and Five New Projects
articles.summary: Brogent Technologies showcased R-Ride at IAAPA Expo Asia 2026 alongside a strategic partnership with a major Japanese IP and five confirmed Southeast Asian venue projects. The 360-degree motion theatre format represents a significant step forward in immersive, high-throughput attraction design. Suppliers and FEC developers should track this platform as a potential global rollout candidate.

articles.title: Red Raion Launches 'Dragon's Gold – The Chase' for 5D, VR, Dome, and Flying Theatre Formats
articles.summary: CGI studio Red Raion has released Dragon's Gold – The Chase, a new licensable attraction film designed for family audiences and available in multiple formats including 5D, VR, dome, and flying theatre. The content features synchronized effects, comedy, and high throughput design, making it a practical addition for FECs looking to refresh existing media-based attractions. Operators with motion theatres or VR setups should explore this as a cost-effective new experience layer.

articles.title: Plymouth, UK to Get Major New Go-Kart and Laser Tag Entertainment Hub
articles.summary: A large new indoor entertainment venue combining go-karts and laser tag is planned for Plymouth, England, reflecting continued investment in multi-attraction FEC formats in the UK. The development follows a broader trend of new FEC openings anchored by racing and combat-style attractions. Operators and developers can benchmark the concept mix and scale against their own venue planning.

articles.title: Tenpin Opens New Entertainment Centre in Blackpool
articles.summary: Tenpin, a UK-based entertainment center chain, has opened a new venue in Blackpool featuring bowling and associated attractions. The opening adds to Tenpin's growing national footprint and signals continued brick-and-mortar investment in the UK FEC market. Operators in the region should note the brand's expansion pace and attraction mix as a competitive reference point.

articles.title: Multi-Million-Pound Entertainment Venue Opens at Brent Cross, London
articles.summary: A new large-scale entertainment venue has opened at Brent Cross shopping center in London, continuing the trend of mall-anchored FEC development in the UK. The multi-million-pound investment reflects landlord and operator confidence in experiential entertainment as a retail traffic driver. FEC developers and investors should monitor this opening for format and performance data.

articles.title: NRG Adventure Park Advances Plans in Jacksonville's Southside Commons
articles.summary: NRG Adventure Park is moving forward with its development in the Southside Commons area of Jacksonville, Florida, signaling growth in the adventure park FEC segment in the Southeast. The project adds to a pipeline of new indoor adventure venues targeting family demographics. Local FEC operators in the Jacksonville market should prepare for new competition.

articles.title: Madison, Mississippi 'The Gallery' Entertainment District Set to Break Ground This Summer
articles.summary: A new entertainment district called The Gallery is set to break ground in Madison, Mississippi, adding a mixed-use FEC and dining destination to the local market. The project reflects growing developer interest in suburban entertainment districts as anchors for mixed-use real estate. FEC investors and operators should track this model as an emerging format in secondary markets.

articles.title: WildBrain CPLG to Drive Location-Based Entertainment for 'Miraculous' Across EMEA and APAC
articles.summary: WildBrain CPLG has been appointed to develop location-based entertainment opportunities for the Miraculous IP across Europe, the Middle East, Africa, and Asia-Pacific. The deal opens new licensing pathways for FEC operators in those regions to incorporate the popular animated brand into attractions and themed experiences. Operators seeking proven family IP for licensing should evaluate Miraculous as a viable option.

articles.title: Chuck E. Cheese Partners with Toy Foundation for International Day of Play with Free Admission Event
articles.summary: Chuck E. Cheese celebrated International Day of Play by offering free Adventure Zone admission and launching a June fundraiser in partnership with the Toy Foundation. The promotion highlights how major FEC chains are leveraging cause-marketing and community engagement to drive traffic and brand goodwill. Independent FEC operators can draw inspiration from this promotional model for their own seasonal campaigns.

articles.title: Three Women Charged After Riot Forces Urban Air in Raleigh to Close Early
articles.summary: A disturbance at an Urban Air Adventure Park in Raleigh, North Carolina resulted in three arrests and forced the venue to shut down early, raising public safety concerns for FEC operators. The incident underscores the ongoing challenge of crowd management and security protocols at high-volume family entertainment venues. Operators should review their security staffing, incident response plans, and communication procedures in light of events like this.

articles.title: Kennywood Announces New All-Day Chaperone Policy
articles.summary: Kennywood amusement park is implementing an all-day chaperone requirement, a policy trend that has significant implications for FEC and amusement venue operators managing youth visitor demographics. The move follows similar policies adopted by other parks in response to safety and behavioral incidents. FEC operators should consider whether similar supervision policies are appropriate for their venues, particularly for teens and youth groups.

articles.title: Play Playground Nashville Launches Monthly Cocktail Program to Drive Repeat Visits
articles.summary: Play Playground Nashville has introduced a monthly rotating cocktail program called 'Playmaker's Pour,' featuring patriotic themed drinks for June and July. The initiative is designed to drive repeat adult visitation and complement the venue's social gaming experience. FEC operators with bar programs should take note of this low-cost, high-engagement strategy for building adult audience loyalty.

articles.title: London-Inspired Social Entertainment Venue with Interactive Games Coming to Paris
articles.summary: A new social entertainment concept featuring foosball, virtual soccer, interactive games, cocktails, and fast food is set to open in Paris, imported from the London market. The venue reflects growing international appetite for adult-oriented social FEC formats that blend gaming with food and beverage. U.S. and European FEC operators should monitor this concept as a benchmark for the evolving social entertainment category.

articles.title: ProSlide Debuts 'PowerRAPIDS' – A New Category of Water Ride – at Vietnam's Aquatopia
articles.summary: ProSlide Technology has premiered PowerRAPIDS at Aquatopia Water Park in Vietnam, introducing a hybrid water ride that combines sliding with floating river channels for sustained thrills. The format is designed to deliver continuous dynamic motion and group adventure, differentiating it from traditional slides. Water park operators and FEC developers with aquatic attractions should evaluate this new ride category for future capital investments.

articles.title: Chamber Members Preview New Bear River Entertainment Zone in Northern California
articles.summary: Business chamber members from Fortuna and Eureka toured the new Bear River entertainment zone, signaling local economic investment in family entertainment in a smaller Northern California market. The development reflects how FEC concepts are expanding into smaller communities beyond major metro areas. Operators and developers targeting underserved markets may find this a useful case study.

articles.title: Menasha's Sabre Lanes Closes, Directing Customers to Appleton Bowling Center
articles.summary: Sabre Lanes in Menasha, Wisconsin has permanently closed, with the ownership directing guests to a related bowling facility in nearby Appleton. The closure highlights the ongoing consolidation and attrition affecting traditional bowling centers in smaller markets. Bowling-anchored FEC operators should consider this a reminder to diversify their attraction mix and revenue streams.

articles.title: Arizona Ranks Its Top 10 Indoor Entertainment Attractions for 2026
articles.summary: AZ Big Media has published its 2026 ranking of the top 10 indoor entertainment attractions in Arizona, providing a useful snapshot of which venues and formats are resonating with consumers in one of the nation's fastest-growing states. The list serves as a competitive intelligence resource for FEC operators in the Southwest. Marketers and developers can use such rankings to benchmark their own venues' visibility and reputation.

articles.title: Expert Guide to Modular Design and Global Logistics for Travelling Exhibitions
articles.summary: Industry experts share best practices for building touring exhibitions, covering modular structural design, freight efficiency with stackable tour carts, and remote media updates via hardware abstraction. The guide is particularly relevant for FEC operators and suppliers developing portable or temporary attractions and pop-up experiences. Key takeaways include using commercial off-the-shelf components and standardizing operational documentation to minimize downtime at each venue.

articles.title: Alton Towers Adds Minecraft 'Meet the Mobs' Pop-Up Experience This Summer
articles.summary: Merlin Entertainments is adding a Minecraft-branded pop-up experience to Alton Towers featuring life-size character encounters, interactive activities, themed snacks, and exclusive merchandise. The activation leverages one of gaming's most recognizable IP for family audiences and includes an in-game reward incentive to drive engagement. FEC operators should note the potential of gaming IP activations as low-capital seasonal attractions.

articles.title: Christie Launches XP Series LED with 10-Year Warranty for Immersive Entertainment Venues
articles.summary: Christie has unveiled its XP Series LED display platform, designed for high-stakes visual environments including immersive entertainment spaces, backed by an industry-leading 10-year warranty. The system offers precise color reproduction, advanced grayscale performance, and flexible deployment options suited to FEC attractions, VR spaces, and themed environments. Operators investing in LED display infrastructure should evaluate this platform's total cost of ownership against the extended warranty benefit.
